title: quassia indica
intro: Quassia indica, also known as Amargoso or Quassia, is a plant species belonging to the family Rutaceae and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant's bark, roots, and leaves possess medicinal properties, containing compounds like quassin, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Quassia indica include its potential to act as an anti-inflammatory, antimalarial, and antihelmintic agent, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of dyspepsia and diarrhea. Quassia indica can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
